resposta:
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def isPowerOfFour(self, n: int) -> bool:
        return n > 0 and (n & (n - 1)) == 0 and (n & 0xAAAAAAAA) == 0
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses bitwise operations to check if the number is a power of four. It first checks if the number is positive, then checks if it is a power of two by verifying that it has exactly one bit set to 1 in its binary representation. Finally, it checks if the set bit is at an even position by using the mask 0xAAAAAAAA, which has bits set to 1 at all odd positions. If all conditions are met, the number is a power of four.
